channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 169 replies — 676.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 169 • Updated: 2026-07-01 18:09 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423880
The reason i keep bringing up DRAM and other return stuff is because it's on my mind to go into that stuff now, sell, and then increase my VOO position even further - not to infinitely chase returns btw

62424100
>>62423880
I'm putting 20k into micron tomorrow. I feel like it's not to late for this to just keep going up. Its not going to 10x but 4-5, yeah...

62424179
>>62424100
why only micron, why not dram etf

62424607
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
I'm in this and doing VT. Maybe I'll be ok probably not. Can do more than $150 though

62424664
I seriously cannot decide between just not thinking and doing broad ETFs and trying to chase this AI thing

62426022
>>62423236
>>62423245
It's a mostly unserious backtest, site was posted earlier >>62419980

>>62423769
Why not have some fun and make the most unhinged etf port possible??

62426103
>>62426022
Interesting thanks

62426138
I have been degenerately long-only various semiconductors since the april bottom and yet on 90% of days, I've underperformed soxl on the way up and outperformed it on the way down. After doing all my research and picking the right names and calculating the right options to buy, I should have thrown all my notes in the trash and just bought soxl. Even if this is the bubble top and soxl is about to go down 90%, I know from experience my stock picks will go down 95%. So I stay may as well buy soxl.

62426144
If I'm just starting out with this stuff, what's wrong with 70% VTI / 30% VXUS?

62426154
Honestly I’m seeing a lot of upside to going into QQQ over VOO as long as you are okay with the fact it is more volatile
The upside it’s just so huge when compounded

62426173
>>62426154
Or honestly, as someone already in VOO, I wonder if it makes sense to just go into something like VGT which has consistently beaten VOO and QQQ (for now anyway) or something like DRAM
A lot of VGTs holdings are currently down but it’s YTD is higher

62426183
>>62407078
I keep half of my funds in VTWAX, rebalancing annually. Right now I'm down 0.84% this year, my portfolio is fucked but VTWAX is my safety net.

62427417
I am also curious about DRAM can anyone tell me if it’s a good hold for a short while

62428031
jfc DRAM (ie a thematic ETF) is bullshit made for retard retail investors that get their financial advice from grifters off of tiktok. this happens every time a finance topic hits the news and every time people get burned.
barely worth saying. if you're unironically taking advice from /biz/ you're already doomed intellectually.

62428054
>>62428031
but why exactly, what reason is there to believe the companies within it won't continue to turn a profit for the foreseeable future?

62428080
>>62428031
>>62428054
Like I'm serious, what exactly is wrong about holding it and selling once memory cools down, is it smarter to just go into a more volatile ETF that at least has a longer history if you want more returns, is it just hype and not really the next big thing, explain why

62428238
>>62428080
this is basic finance 101, there are plenty of resources online or off that will explain it to you.
ignorance isn't even the issue here, your reaction to not knowing something is to have random retards on a thinly veiled gambling board tell you what to think. and don't just jump on another grifter selling a course or ask some AI slop engine and ask it leading questions like "why is investing in DRAM a good idea?".

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

62430693
VOO anons where you at?

62430902
>>62400493
Contrarian nerds always lose.
Always.
In middle school dating.
Being high school incels.
In college.
At work.
In love.
At investing.
At all times.
Follow the crowd and try to fit in. Everyone doing anything else is a reject loser.

62431131
>>62430902
Who is being the contrarian here though

62431374
VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses, Michelle Louie & Aurélie Denis. So with your 0.03% management fee you're essentially paying for those do-nothing "day in the life of a portfolio manager" videos for daughters of elite sociopaths.

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

item.title: Anyone else stacking cardboard? It's the smartest play you can be making right now - the prices are crazy, like BAYC 5 y… — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else stacking cardboard? It's the smartest play you can be making right now - the prices are crazy, like BAYC 5 y…

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-07-01 18:15 UTC

OP

Anyone else stacking cardboard? It's the smartest play you can be making right now - the prices are crazy, like BAYC 5 years ago, except cardboard is actually going to continue to moon forever.

Early replies (first 3)

62428300
People said this about comic books in the 60s, 70s, and 80s, and then it all came crashing down in the 90s. Modern aesthetics don't age well.

62428317
>>62428300
Cards have something that comics, Beanie Babies, and Pez dispensers didn't have: modern social media to peddle unlicensed financial advice.

62428336
>>62428288
classic bubble

as soon as boomers die and younger generations get kicked out of the house you're going to see this stuff crash real quick

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62428355
>>62428347
>exponential gains
you don't seem to understand this term

62428357
>>62428348
Tbf, they were worthless for a century (until the 1980s) and weren't beating traditional assets until late 2020.

That being said, it's only up from here.

62428358
>>62428348
>trading cards have been around since the 1880's,
this is true, but the vast majority of 1880's trading cards are still worthless.

62428360
>>62428288
I like some of the old designs and those cards are common & cheap. Not paying more than $2 for a piece of 20 year old cardboard but good luck to those who do.

62428379
>>62428288
I remember when entire binders of this set (fireworks holo ony) were being sold for about $300. I thought that, if I waited, I would find it at a lower price. No way am I going to spend hundreds of thousands for the same thing. There needs to be someone willing to print replicas of this exact holo pattern, but with quality card backing. I personally missed out, I started collecting around Skyridge. Then, the tcg pretty much died out here.

62428388
>>62428358
>>62428357
Trading cards didn't become great until the 1952 Topp's baseball set, up until then 1880's-1951 they didn't even try with the cards
One of the main reasons Pokemon TCG blew up a few years ago was the introduction of IR and SIR's, full art rares. Pokemon finally started making the cards into actual art-work and people loved it, it will only continue to grow.
One Piece TCG also blew up for same reason, their rare's are beautiful to look at irl, the top tier cards shine like it has jewels imbedded, and the artwork is amazing.
It is not a fad or anything, they actually started caring about the artwork and people responded, just like baseball cards blew up in 1952 when they started trying

62428427
>>62428388
what what a post

its a bubble everyone trading cards will collapase within 365 days sell ur shit close ur shops it's time to move on

62428431
>>62428348
Not buying your basedllennial bags, poketard.

62428441
>>62428431
>>62428427
COME SEE MY CARDS

62428462
>>62428388
>up until then 1880's-1951 they didn't even try with the cards
this is perhaps one of the dumbest takes I've read on the topic ever.

But then I actively collect and sell 1800's trading cards so I'm a bit biased.

62428468
>>62428462
you're right, I was exaggerating, I don't know much about old baseball cards
Is it a good time to buy 1800's cards? They seem really cheap, most of them are 50-100 dollars from what I see

62428635
>>62428468
most 1800's cards aren't sports cards

but no, I don't expect them to go up in value much after this long. Not because they're ugly or low effort, but because almost nobody collects them.

62428679
buy vintage cards. psa 9s and ungraded.

62429112
>>62428635
>but no, I don't expect them to go up in value much after this long.

Not necessarily. All trading cards were worthless for the first century of their existence. The people who were babies when trading cards were invented were dead by the time they started becoming valuable. So cards from the 1880s could absolutely moon.

62429122
>>62428300
but comic books are gay and cards (of basically any type) are inherently cool

62429151
>>62429122
People thought the exact opposite 40 years ago. You are unlikely to do well long-term with art and entertainment as a store of value unless it translates really well across eras and cultures.

62429383
the entire pokemon market was pumped beyond belief and had unique reasons to be worth lots of money

62429525
Would unironically be a better play to just buy sealed booster packs and leave them in your garage for 20 years.

62429966
>>62428288
unironically got into it coz of that zard tard who posts in /biz/ every day

62432323
In the 2021 bull run I roundtripped like a dummy but the one smart thing I did was buy pokemon with some of my bags.

I bought the card in that pic for not much money. It’s HP condition so it won’t get a 10 (most Legendary Collection reverse Holos won’t, they’re notorious for being easily damaged) but the 1st edition Sabrina’s Gengar is pretty damn good condition, LP at worst. I paid $175 for that one and I paid $120 for the Psyduck Scream promo. Both are worth four figures now.

62432473
>>62428288
He borted cardboard ?

62434436
>>62428288
Why does everyone refer to card pumping as "the hobby"? Feels like one of those "we know what we're doing is evil as shit so we're gonna use a euphemism" situations. Like how mafia orgs refer ti themselves as "the family business".

62434462
>>62428288
How do I literally invest in cardboard? Plastic packaging is going to get banned hard.

62434494
>>62434462
Buy, throw it in a closet for 5 years, then sell. It's the most autopilot asset with exponential growth potential in the world.

62434659
Real $

62434682
>>62434659
>5k for some gay dude farting

62434739
>>62434682
Literal Olympians hit this pose when they win. You wouldn't know anything about this, as a chud loser

62435313
zoomzooms and millennifags are kinda like gen-z and boomers with real estate when it comes to modern collectibles. They can only remember line going up and have not learned from historical trends.
All modern collectibles are junk and a cash grab. Pokelimans, magik the gardening are printed in insane number like the 90s wax era, there is a ton of that stuff stored away and once a downturn in the economy or the consumer base reaches a certain age and lose interest the market will become saturated with stuff that nobody wants because at the end of the day these things have no real value. You can't create anything with it apart from fire.

62435396
>>62428635
>but no, I don't expect them to go up in value much after this long. Not because they're ugly or low effort, but because almost nobody collects them.
There has to be hype and people have to know about them. They need to be reminded they exist for them to be sought after and thus a buyers market. It is fascinating really when you have been in the business for a while and you come across something really rare, special or even unique. It may be cool AF and completely unobtainable yet nobody wants to pay anything for it because there is absolutely nobody that knows anything about it. For instance a while back I had a sealed triple AAA big box PC game from the late 90s which had extra goodies such as the soundtrack on a separate CD recorded by the Moscow symphony orchestra which in itself is probably ultra rare, but in the end I did not get much for it because nobody had seen the damn product, it wasn't even registered on the net and certainly not on the price checking platforms.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 193 replies — 125.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 193 • Updated: 2026-07-01 16:50 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849
>>62423064
This shit is way too schizo. No anon you can not predict the future. You can maybe foresee some type of market trends, seasonality or whatever but this is just ridiculous

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

62429207
>>62428797
But you're still under $10k?

62429246
>>62378965
Anon Belgium is literally doing very well in the world cup, you should make more of these predictions

62431586
bump.

62431599
probably nothing
bio protocol desci ai BIO now cookin with open ai, nvidia, anthropic, claude news check twita

62432055
>>62373752
Hey
I just started out in this crypto thing and bought $450 worth of solana. How do I become cool and multiply it

62432088
>>62432055
Be like me and listen to people within a group of other retards that are pushing a token. The last one tanked a $200 investment -98% but then he re-deployed the same token and it yielded a 40x from where it was called.

62432306
>>62432088
pardon my incompetence but I didn't understand much of what you just wrote anon

62432350
>>62428797
I had bad experiences following twitter people and waiting for signals even those that seem smart. It can be interesting but you’ll be lost if they randomly stop posting, they’re not funds giving you alpha but people that should have better things to do than post if their full job is actually trading.

I do still like @donalt and @cryptocred their show technical roundup is a rare non clickbaity show that just focuses on TA but most people are inherently frauds on social media

62432360
>>62432055
I don’t think Solana is a good buy right now it’s barely holding support and likely going lower to like 40 if I remember correctly

>>62432088
If you have a group like that be a hero to the community and share it to us

62432373
>>62432360
>be a hero to the community and share it to us
see
>>62385756
I recently held an airdrop for /biz/ too

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62424715

62434369
Bump, I'm getting an early weekend because of the country's birthday. Will devote time to this thread.

62435112
>>62432350
skill issue. donalt? lol
you gotta follow the small accounts anon

item.title: sony is stopping disc releases for PS games you know what that means — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
sony is stopping disc releases for PS games you know what that means

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-07-01 18:17 UTC

OP

sony is stopping disc releases for PS games
you know what that means

Early replies (first 3)

62434070
Believe it or not -- calls.
(/v/ermin might disagree)

62434082
>>62434061
So they're using GTA as the 800 lb gorilla to strong arm this through.

There's literally no reason not to abandon consoles completely and go full PC at this point. And I was a diehard Sonybro a few years ago. Then one day a lightbulb clicked, no new gens to buy, no backwards compatibility issues, I can play all my games in one place, with superior graphics, forever.

Yeah fuck this noise.

62434586
>>62434061
Every company:
>how can we gradually make our products worse?
Why though?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434618
>own nothing

62434620
>>62434061
Does it mean that SSDs are going to be more expensive?

Because if you don't know already, most of the playstation games are already "code discs", which means they don't even include the game and you can't play it without downloading a patch and has been like this for years.

62434693
>>62434061
Seems like scraping the barrel for savings. Might boost some interner provider's numbers a bit? I dunno.

62434712
>>62434586
when DEI retards are in management, they always do this, i never saw any manager focus on making product better, they always want to make it cheaper

62434713
short GME

62434731
>>62434061
unironically invest in physical discs

rare ps5 games, ideally ones taken down from store too (but they already pump fast)

62434737
>>62434731
like that deadpool ps4 one they lost rights too, easy 10x in price

62434748
>>62434061
>you know what that means
OH MY GLORP! THEY GOT RID OF THE HECKING DISCOPOPS!

62434831
>>62434620
yes

62434840
>>62434061
This is actually bullish. This means less cost to manufacture discs

62434843
>>62434082
Fuck yeah bro let's switch to PC! All of those games have physical discs and all towers have disc drives!

62434864
>>62434712
It's not DEI management you retard. It's literally capitalism and the need to increase profits every quarter. Browns have nothing to do with this.

62434884
>>62434061
Lmao who the fuck still buys vidya in 2026. You have piracy. There's about 10,000 solid vidya to play over the past 40 years. Download an emulator and get on it faggots

62434892
>>62434082
get all your roms and emulators and back it up and keep one offline emulator PC/laptop for the future.

62434906
>>62434737
>just baghold the shitty $50 game for 10+ years to have that 1 greasy mystery meat mf trying to negrotiate it down to treedfiddy bro

62435107
>>62434731
I threw away like 2 grand of ps3 game discs because I just don't care

62435164
>>62434864
That's not capitalism tho, that's gov't regulation

62435169
Online games are easier to monetize. It's simple economics. Look how much money GTA online has made in the past decade selling DLC to kids.

62435290
>>62435107
rip

62435322
>>62435164
No anon. Government regulations are a good thing and help the consumer. Do you not remember when Apple was forced to drop their proprietary charging cables in favor of USB-C? That was government regulations that did that. Or don't tell me that you'd rather be forced to get new proprietary charging cables with every new phone you got?

62435373
>>62435322
Government regulation is literally what forces companies to disregard investing in their employees and the long term in favor of chasing higher quarterly profits for their investors, which is what that post was a complaint about.

62435392
>>62435373
Goverments USED to do that when they actually had taxes in the 70s and earlier, all the extra declared profits would just get taxed so the companies were forced to either spend it on themselves or give it away, and they spent it on themselves through reinvestment and employees.
Then Reagan happened and "trickle down economics" was sold to the masses that if we just cut regulations, businesses will still spend on themselves instead of chasing profits. Cutting regulations led to heaps of shitty bad loans being spread out through the mortgage market causing the 08 collapse lol.
You're a clown.
Lol.

62435404
>>62434061
> trends in consumer preference

item.title: High IQ thread — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
High IQ thread

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-07-01 18:21 UTC

OP

What's the point of "making it"™ when sex doesn't even feel that good? Think about it. When was the last time you got addicted to pussy like it was heroin? Probably never. The idea seems interesting in your head, but when you're actually having sex, you just want it to end it and go home already. Post-nut clarity confirms that society lied to you that sex being the ultimate pleasure and you fell for it. You probably stumble over the same rock again and again for years until you realize the truth eventually. So, why make millions to have more opportunities for something like...sex? Millions are not going to cure your autism, misogyny or social anhedonia anyways

Early replies (first 3)

62434090
>>62434057
It will solve the wagie and Lambo problems though, which are the only problems I care about. I’d also like to live in a nice house on the lake and ride jet skis all summer.

62434105
>only cares about making it for muh sex
Sex obsession is a massive sign of being low IQ

62434165
>>62434090
Why even own a Lambo? It's an overpriced vehicle made by (((Italians))) you probably won't use more than once or twice a month. Once you make it you will not have a job or nowhere to go, really. And all for what? So you can flex your wealth to some negros and normalfags? You can't ride a car like that without exposing yourself as a target for thieves/murderers/golddiggers. Think about the costs: manteinance, insurance, taxes. It's a lot of money down the drain. Same goes for the jet ski. You say you'll ride it all summer but it will likely be gathering dust for most of the year. It will be a constant headache. You'll need to hire people to clean it, fix whatever problems arise, you need a place to store those toys, more and more dust and cleaning. Unless you're a 80 IQ retard I don't see anyone riding a jet ski for more than a day

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434174
>>62434165
You may be right about the Lambo but you’re wrong about the jet skis I NEED A JET SKI AND I WOULD RIDE IT EVERY DAY AND BANG UR MOM ON IT EVERY NIGHT

62434190
>>62434174
If you make it you can have my sister too, mr jet ski

62434200
The chase of getting a woman to have sex with you is more interesting than the sex itself

62434232
>>62434057
Because the sex isn’t with a woman who has genuine burning desire for you within the bounds of holy matrimony.

62434234
>>62434200
>the chase
You mean talking to a woman? Holy fuck have you ever actually done this? They have no interest in the deep matters of life such as existence, meaning, or spiritual truths. They want to talk about celebrities, gossip, and tv shows. I hate women.

62434249
>>62434234
>I hate women.
you should try men then, fag.

62434322
>>62434232
>muh attraction muh sex in marriage
Women are brainwashed to experience sex as the ultimate pleasure as well. In reality, women are like beautiful and pure temples that feel they want to be desecrated. Except this temple will do everything in its power to make your cock hard so you desecrate it. Therefore the rape fantasies. Nymphos don't necessarily enjoy sex either. Most of them were just molested when they were children/early teens and they seek validation from men by being submissive and slutty. That's it. Sex make women feel guilty in the end. Plus, unless you're chad, no woman will ever feel sexual magnetism for you.
t. I look like quasimodo, its a hard life folks... don't give up, though

62434326
>>62434249
Coomerbrain simp faggot
Wish I could curb stomp your bitch ass

62434332
>>62434057
Making it has nothing to do with sex, idk where you're getting that from. I want to be able to live comfortable, in a comfortable home, not have to wageslave while being able to afford heating cooling, a premium bed, good food, etc.

62434333
Sex without love is useless. Especially if you are female. Guys post sexual revolution are just now finding this out? Must be all the fat chicks nowadays. Yeash! That is what it took. An obesity epidemic. Just date a fat sow and go for long walks to slim her down before having sex. If you fall in love while and if she loses weight then it will be better. If she is not willing to keep up dump her. This should be the male test for a woman. Beats straight up paying a fat hooker.

By the way I did this to some new fat female friends. Not to be mean but because I know obesity a social contagion as well a personal problem. This is a good personality test. If they want to keep up with you with positive encouragement then they have good character. If they try to drag you down into their habits then they have a bad character. Even just for non sexual friendship fat people will do either.

62434420
>>62434057
Buy expensive gift for the whore since sex doesn’t even worth it. Then the whore gold digger would goes on into dating site to look for more “look for right person to start the relationship again”

62434443
>>62434420

62434450
>>62434165
A nice watch will always make you feel fucking great.

Judicious cosmetic surgery is the same. Don't go beyond what's natural, but nothing gives more happiness per dollar spend than a nosejob if your nose is shit or a hair transplant if you have a bald spot. Keep your expectations realistic of course.

62434536
>>62434332
Most people in this filipino board want money so they can have more opportunities to have sex. I just wanted to point out to these people that it's stupid to "make it" so you can have tons of sex when it's not even that addictive (think fentanyl levels of addiction), but what do I know, I'm a schizoid who doesn't feel pleasure from anything and maybe sex is a subjective experience. But we all know what post-nut clarity is so there must be some echo of reason in what i'm saying.
>>62434450
I'm 40 autistic neet that did a shitton of psychedelics. Believe me, a watch means nothing to me. I cut my own hair, dress like a modern day jester, use different socks in my feet. All is vanity, my friend. Chasing the wind

62434702
I just don't want to turn up to work and spend my day getting my soul drained before collapsing on the bed afterwards and doing it all over again the next day.
Simple as.
I've never had a gf and never will so I don't care about any of that stuff.

62434844
>>62434536

62434890

62435009

62435055

It's not different from having an extra chromosome and brag about of it, anyways do a 360 and go be a high t alpha faggot somewhere else, this is a low t socially awkward filipino forum, friend

62435410

62435419
>>62435410

The Polish chick had a more kino and clean pussy tho, and she made me cum fast even wearing a condom. She had the absolute top vagina. Picture perfect. Clean. Pink. Beautiful. Tight. Slick.
She also orgasmed almost immediately upon insertion and then constantly while fucking.

item.title: Trump fucked you — 19 replies — 29.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Trump fucked you

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-07-01 17:44 UTC

OP

>His annual disclosure for 2025 with the US Office of Government Ethics, disclosed that Trump made $636 million in royalties from CIC Digital, LLC, a company that issues $TRUMP, a crypto token that he launched three days before taking office for his second term. Almost all of the come came in the form of royalties from a license agreement with Celebration Coins. Trump's meme coin reached its peak value of $74.24 within a day of launching. On Tuesday evening, its price was $1.67, down 98% since inception.

>The president also reported almost $600 million in income from token sales by World Liberty Financial, a cryptocurrency company backed by the president and his family, and around $65 million from equity sales in the firm that controls World Liberty Financial. Another $196 million in equity sales of Stablecoin Holdco LLC are also listed. A crypto firm and issuer of tokens, World Liberty Financial was launched during the 2024 presidential campaign. It was co-founded by the president, his sons Eric and Donald Jr. and Zack and Alex Witkoff, the sons of U.S. special envoy and longtime Trump friend Steve Witkoff.

Eternal aristocratic levels of wealth were transferred from plebs directly to Trump's family via shitcoin scams.

Early replies (first 3)

62433591
>>62433580
>Zack and Alex Witkoff, the sons of U.S. special envoy and longtime Trump friend Steve Witkoff.
I recently learned what background this family has. I was naive to think Witkoff wasnt.....
>>>62433555

62433599
>>62433580
>>62433591
Imagine his sons short when he is cooperating with the cz cartel 1 minute before his retarded tweets and cex dumps! Its just the top of the (((berg)))

62433604
>>62433599

FOUR... FIVE HUNDRED? MAYBE SIX HUNDRED?
GET RAPED IN YOUR SAGGY BUTTHOLE CRYPTO KEKS

62433624
>>62433618

62433629
>>62433618

62433632

62433672
>>62433632

I of course would have aggressively sold the way up and dumped all at lower then 50. Would have made so much money. So no, people buying at the first day to dump high were not dumb.

62433743
>>62433672

Trumpenberg would be thankful for your contribution (he wouldn't be).

62433763

62433764

62433798
>>62433763

62433862

62433864

62433962

And even though I didn’t actually buy any of his shitcoins, I still paid for it because it sucked all the liquidity up and then he dumped on everyone, cratering the entire market and single handedly wrecking the bull run. Then he wrecked it again with tariffs. Then he wrecked it again with the Iran war. I don’t think I’ll be voting again. Fuck the government.

>The filing shows more than $500 million from World Liberty Financial and over $600 million from meme coin sales through CIC Digital. Trump also earned millions more from sales of branded Bibles, sneakers, and watches.

>The disclosure highlights how Trump’s newer business ventures are generating more revenue than parts of his traditional real estate empire.

>Are you surprised by how much Trump made outside of real estate?

item.title: hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai… — 20 replies — 19.7/hr

OP

hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gains. and what a delicious treat to see when i returned: link struggling to stay above $7 and fat serg canceling the build program. LMAO. you stinkoids just can't catch a break huh. honestly how do ya'll have any hope left at this point. what is going to cause link to break $10, let alone $50? kekkerinos.

62424357
>>62424306
We've missed you. :(

62424375
After a big pump and dump, assets seldom pump again. It happened once with BTC but it's rare. Link is unlikely to see serious gains again.

62424382
>>62424375
The big banks will pump our bags once they start using LINK in earnest by 2030 to run the entire financial infrastructure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425397
>>62424306

Fellow link holders we should just ignore this guy.

He is spreading fud about our investment and he is wrong

Can't the mods just ban him?

I mean I know this is 4chan and all and we like to joke and be mean to one another but as a multiple year long chainlink holder I have my limits

Anyway i'm back to my job cleaning toilets now

62425406

i get great pleasure knowing it just gets worse and worse for you as time goes on,


i get great pleasure knowing your lasts breaths will be that you sadly had to pass away before link pumped

item.title: So I freshly graduated as a ME, and I applied to a job posting last friday, not expecting much since the position sounde… — 5 replies — 5.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
So I freshly graduated as a ME, and I applied to a job posting last friday, not expecting much since the position sounde…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-01 17:31 UTC

OP

So I freshly graduated as a ME, and I applied to a job posting last friday, not expecting much since the position sounded too fantastic. This monday though, they sent me applicability tests (IQ + Personality test) that I did a lot better with than I thought I could. An hour from turning the tests in, I got a phone call and it was the company wanting to do a preliminary interview. They basically were pleased with my questions and answers during the call, and we even agreed about the salary I would receive. The next step is an interview on friday, which I assume is final. So everything looks pretty good, but is there any way that I could still fuck it up? I've been familiarizing myself with the specific work the position involves, and what the company is specialized on in general. Basically, should I temper my expectations?

Early replies (first 3)

62433156
Got any more Fresco memes? They’re my fav

62433159
>>62433156
This is the only one I have saved :( I couldn't find more

62433596
>>62433156

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434093
>>62433153
>should I temper my expectations
yes. they probably have several others they're interviewing (or have already interviewed). that said, if you really nail the in-person interview then you may well walk out of there with a start date.

62435236
>>62433153
Interviews are like dates. You have to seem lightly into it, but not too too interested. Like you'd be fine either way. Maybe you'll take it, whatever, you don't care. Good luck to you, my fellow MEdiocrity. I wish you all the best. Make sure to not stay at your first job longer that 2 years, 3 if you really like it. And you want to make your way into controls engineering eventually. Get your state license if you can. If they have someone you can work under.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-01 17:32 UTC

62434113

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 52 replies — 39.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-07-01 17:03 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62430298
>>62428790
those arent futures contracts. they are not bulletproof. a company does not have to deliver and the company does not have to buy. they can deliberate about how much was stolen for years and by the time they reach a conclusion the market has already moved on. fundis look good right now mem-fag. go for it. but expect rapid unexplained drop in value somewhere near term then they will go under, aquired by private equity, ect. purpose built hardware incased in a software platform will be the norm becuase that will be the only way to afford rapid change in what is considered efficient. the days of radioshack at the mall are almost over.

62430354
>>62430298
Well what do you suggest
I am a noob but everything I read makes it seem like Memory is here to stay, production is only increasing
Is it just a matter of consumer demand not dropping in that case if these contracts are not set in stone?

62430446
>>62430354
go long software. the contrarian goes long on hardware. its not a safe bet. all that shit has to be changed out much faster than it did in the early 2000s. it gets thrown into a shipping container and shipped to a somolian trash pile to be sorted out and burned in a pot for the metals. it looks fucking great right now, people rode the upside of the arbitage between the time it takes them to adopt their own methods. each one of these companies is gonna adopt the apple mentality but it will be with chipsets and manufacturing. we used to live in a world where it came from china or a little island with the blueprints. that is not the case anymore. the hardware isnt even the biggest cost for the AI giants.

62430659
>>62427323

62432266
.

62432668
>>62427323
Semiconductor funds have been crazy profitable and seem to only be increasing. The real money is in selling dumbass AI companies the hardware for their ultimately doomed datacenter investments.

62432824
>>62432668
Do you think Semis > Memory?

62432833
Semis will crash hard once China catches up. Not interesting in that ticking time bomb

62432934
>>62432833
Is memory a safe bet then
DRAM

62433058
>>62430446
>go long on the sector that is still dumping
>don't invest in picks and shovels sector that has no end in sight and has insane earnings reports one after another
I wouldn't listen to this guy.

62433271
>>62427323
Obviously, smart people invested in this in 2024, or last year at the latest. Now is the time to sell, obviously. How is that not obvious to you? Are you a shill? Are there SHILLS and BAGHOLDERS on 4chan?

62433313
>>62432824
>>62432934
Look at these questions made of pure retail last leg.

62433362
>>62427323
rumor of new architecture causing fear for the memory final solution
careful with your longs anons

62433597
>>62433313
What makes you say this

62433722
>>62427339
>you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's
??????????? The hell are you talking about ABSOLUTE retard? DRAM companies are about manufacturing RAM sticks.

62433745
you fucking cunts /smg/ has been telling you to buy Micron for MONTHS now and you morons are still holding on to your crypto bullshit

62433773
>>62427323
>How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
30/70 imo. When you understand the tech underlying this and its actual implementations in every industry, you start to understand that it's genuinely not a bubble, it's the biggest productivity increase since computing but is happening at a much faster rate than computer adoption. If anything the markets are currently UNDERvaluing these stocks because retail investors (and smaller institutions) can't understand the tech.

Memory companies are a decent mid-term hold but after a couple years we will see a simultaneous drop in demand and increase in supply and their margins will collapse back to normal levels - I plan to divest in 12-18 months.

Long term I foresee Anthropic and especially OpenAI being the big winners. Like, 90s Apple levels of winning. These two have an iron grip on flagship LLM tech and frankly can charge whatever the fuck they want for enterprise-level applications and flagship API access. Google and X are the closest to matching them but both are 1-2 years behind if we're being generous. Chinks (and non-Americans in general) are so far behind it's comical. Certain big tech companies (notably Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta, not necessarily in that order) are also poised to win big thanks to their adoption and utilization strategies; Microsoft had a big league but has kind of dropped the ball, but I don't think any of those 3 are going to be the big success that OpenAI/Anthropic will be.

TSMC and NVDA are both excellent long term holds, NVDA arguably moreso (and I say this while being someone who thinks the chances of a CCP invasion, ever, are ~0) but both are great, if a bit overbought in the short term. When I divest from MU etc, TSMC and NVDA are where the cash is most likely to end up.

62433786
Unironically
If someone wanted more safety is SOXX a better bet? I know it’s Semi Conductor focused and not RAM
It is still up triple digits percentage wise over the last year and has a longer track record of 25 years
Is it a smart investment for an overall AI Satellite account versus something thematic like DRAM? Also considering BAKA but don’t know much about it
This is for someone investing over time, short or medium term is fine, I just mean not a giant lump sum

62433799
>>62433773
Okay your claim about betting on Memory through 2027 (12-18 months) feels in line with everything I’ve read
What do you plan on doing to get exposure to memory just something like DRAM or a different option
I’m fine with most of my long term holds just being broad ETFs instead of only betting on tech, but meanwhile short to medium I’m unsure if I take more risk

62433801
>>62433786
I'm not really sure since not all semiconductor companies are created equal. A lot of them are specialized in ways that aren't conducive to AI and while they're completely fine as long-term stable investments, that's not why you or anyone else is investing in SOXX. Personally I'd pick and choose.

62433818
>>62433799
I'm pretty basic and generally more risk averse than other tech bulls so I just sank a bunch of money into MU, WDC, STX, and SNDK, no leverage. If MU hits 1600 I might sell since that's being cited as a potential ceiling by a large number of analysts and frankly the sentiment is more important than any technical validity in this time frame imo. Also fwiw VGT is the second largest position in my Roth IRA but I view that as a 10+ year hold.

62433837
>>62433818
I just have a giant share of VOO from DCA my paychecks into it without thinking but kinda felt like that was wasting a big opportunity now
Hard to say it without is sounding like FOMO cause it’s not
Just looking for a smart hold that should beat the market average that I can sell and then just circle it all back into VOO and chill, I’m nearing 6 figures in it as is which is my goal for this year

62434663
.

62434778
DRAM Down 10% today
Is this a buy?
Trading at $67 currently

62434788
it's over, memory is gay and bankrupt

62434794
>>62434788
You say as Micron just signed another deal this time with GM

62434941
>>62430659
Same.

62435104
>>62433362
i tried to warn you...

62435154
>>62434778
I am also curious
Is this a buy or maybe wait for 20% down

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 126 replies — 38.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 126 • Updated: 2026-07-01 15:07 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424386
>>62422668
It literally says "the most recent season of Chainlink Rewards will mark the conclusion of Build-related token rewards, with claims ending on July 7, 2026" meaning no season 2+ of build rewards going to stakers.

It also says "Proceeds from these new agreements are expected to be programmatically converted to LINK and used to support the growth of the network through programs such as the Chainlink Reserve." meaning that link goes to the reserve, not to stakers.

Previously
>stakers will get build rewards

Now
>Chainlink gets paid in link

62424528
xswap isnt even on cow swap? where do you sell this trash?

62424728
S E X

62424821
>>62424386
Brutal

62425620
>>62424023
>this project has always been pushed by morons
>also I've been holding this project for years

You're lying. And brown.

>>62424386
You're a brown ESL monkey who doesn't even know what the words "such as" mean.
Protip: the same exact words were used when Build was first introduced.

62425675
>>62417503
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA Another nail in the coffin and shovel full of dirt in the grave on the biggest retail fraud in token history.

62425681
>>62419638
Link is underperforming fucking death spiraled LUNC since Trump revived crypto. Lol And by a lot.

62425687
>>62419707
>A chainlink shill calling someone else brown.

62425699
>>62422159
>>62423276
Perfectly said.

62425962
>>62425620
I'm a 100k link holder. I have more link than you.

"Such as" isn't very fucking reliable. All of this would be fine if link was valued as it should be but it fucking isn't, with no clear path to gaining that value.

62425980
>>62425962
>CLL uses "such as" in 2022 when launching Build
>THIS IS SET IN STONE

>CLL uses "such as" in 2026 when changing Build
>THIS IS NOT VERY FUCKING RELIABLE

You're a brown paid fuddie.

62426004
>>62425980
I'm also whiter than you, Yank.

62426010
>>62425980
>>62425620
post a picture of your hand with timestamp

62426949
i am just getting caught up on this news after the weekend. my my my how the mighty have fallen

i predicted all of this for the last few years but the boyscouts couldn't handle it

>duuuuuuuuuuuude i need a team of tax attorneys for the heckin rewards

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAH

look at you now. an expensive lesson on controlling your ego. you needed it tho


your hubris was your downfall

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62426967
>>62417512
>There are thousands of other coins.

LOL this dude stuck in 2021. that's your conclusion bro, just buy other scam coins?

don't you people get it? altcoins are dead. capital moved back to actual companies in the stock market. only a retarded sap would continue donating to a bunch of nerds for a "whitepaper"


>>62417833
>I'll also (again just playing the other side) point out the regardless of whether or not it ultimately benefits the LINK token, Chainlink is realistically one of like 5 crypto projects amongst the hundreds of "legitimate" ones that actually is seeing real adoption and has a huge use case that is surviving the infantile stages of crypto, and not just surviving but will thrive. So again, who knows if this ultimately helps the token price, but I do understand why linkies hold onto some faith still.

so are you just a fan of the company?

>>62417873
>It’s all push and pull. Ying yang. If you have 20 million earning you 800k (500k post tax) per year

who the fuck would do that? the $20m has drawn down 80% so no, life is not good. anyone not retarded would've parked most of that in the s&p500 and has been making at least 15% per year

if you really wanted pathetic 4% return you could get that in a savings account where your only risk is that actual bank going bust

62427007
>>62418846
> The opportunity to invest in something like Chainlink - core infrastructure with insane value capture potential, has never really existed before. The closest thing maybe would be investing in AMZN prior to cloud services,

you are not invested in chainlink. you made a donation kid

amazon's revenue in year 7 was $3B. and that's in 2001 numbers. you can go ahead and stop comparing to your shitcoin bro you're embarrassing yourself

>>62420893
>The fuck is that analogy lmao. This isn't a casino you dumb faggot, real people invested real money into an asset that promised certain things by certain dates and they have not only underdelivered but are starting to actively ignore those promises.

it's the end stage of a cult investment where they turn on each other. it somehow becomes the fault of the "community" on why they didnt make any money.

in order to preserve their ego, guys like >>62420825 have to spin the story that you'd have to be crazy to expect any kind of gains. mind you the rest of the world's market all hit all time highs while you are down 80-90%. by creating this narrative, he avoids recognizing he's holding a really bad investment and can continue on thinking he's a secret genius.

62427022
remember this old pasta? still relevant today. every cl product roughly follows this life cycle

62429766
>>62425681
Oh I remember this one - this poster is an actual bot.

62431562
>>62419888
The best part is SIR GAY NIGGEROV got all these companies to be part of the BUILD program, and then those companies did actually in many cases issue tokens but most did not provide tokens to CLL to give out as rewards for participating in the Staking pool. If there had actually been airdrops from every single participant then the program would have brought some relevant value to token holders instead of what, like 9 companies giving tokens long after their tokens had already followed the shitcoin chart to its completion and become totally worthless.

62431631
soon the staking program will be canceled too
and then stinkies will be out of all hope
feels good

62432093
>>62431631
Well the new line seems to be the price will improve when all link is circulating and the team has fully divested itself of the token. Which i don't know if it's just late stage imbecile drivel or the most brazen shill line ever attempted in history.

62432101
>>62425620
I was making Teagan memes at the san fran meet up in 2019. I honestly wish it was otherwise but I've wasted fucking years with link.

62432489
>>62417503
Bullish for LINK

62433501
Why are the fuddies always the ones with the most posts?

62433680
>>62423312
First of all there are no vacations until the wife gives you kids.

62433701
>>62427022
checked and /thread

62434455
>>62433501
its what they're paid for, the number of posts

62434545
>>62433501
>>62434455

It’s obvious that they are paid to do this; what I don’t get is whether they’re doing it to get midwit like me to buy (and I bought without regrets), or to get dumbs to sell.

62434643
>>62434545
*sell/stay away

62434699
>>62433501
>>62434545
>blatant samefagging
why are shillers such grifting and disingenuous retards?

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 51 replies — 26.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-07-01 16:27 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427891
>>62427359
Tnx for that reminder boss

62429392
>>62426552
Because we're asking. What's wrong??

62429688
We will keep holding

62429720
>>62427357
You really know what you're talking about

62429723
Not buying but riding out the 'storm'. There have been much worse times.

62430182
>>62429723
Ride well

62430234
need to sell enough today to pay rent and bills

62430266
>>62430234
Don't sell

62431888
>>62429688
You can always hold, or stake it if you want to earn a bit while waiting.

62431895
>>62426626
depends on which country for sure

62431900
>>62426512
I go in at $100K. Every $20 I spend BTC drops another $1,000.

62431909
My plan is, quite simply, not to own shitcoin

62431916
>>62431909
Like memecoins???!!!

62431991
>>62431888
not possible
>>62431900
Spend every day

62432200
Thoughts on ansem everyone

62433067
>>62426733
Btc is memecoin? Wow

62433595
>>62431991
It's possible. Babylon already supports native BTC staking, if you haven't seen it yet.

62433598
>>62426638
We'll see how it plays out. Good luck either way.

62433616
The elites are finished with Bitcoin. It has served their purpose.

62433621
>>62433616
is bobo based on Shane Gillis? I can't unsee it.

62433746
>>62433595
is this real?

62433751
>>62433616
you know nothing at all

62433844
>>62433598
Back at ya anon. Wishing you much WAGMI

62433854
>>62426512
I'm not retarded so I'm never buying BTC for any price. Being a cryptotard in 2026 is even more pathetic than being a /pmg/tard

62433884
>>62433854
Your lack of retardness coupled with no faith in yourself is why you will never WAGMI

62433961
>>62426512
I don't think about BTC. It was created by centralized exchanges to attack Bitcoin. You're only talking about it because they printed a bunch of tether to get the price to $100k. There are no users, only a small group of poor speculators paying actual USD for this coin. The rest of it is tether iou's.

62434984
>>62433746
Yeah, it's real. Go look into it if you're skeptical. They even have their TBVs in public testnet now.

62434990
>>62433844
Twins?! Who

62434999
>>62433844
Twins?! Who's your bet? Lol

62435029
>>62433961
If there were really no users, I don't think we'd still be seeing institutions, ETFs, and developers building around Bitcoin.
